text: This is the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: 这是美国之音特别英语健康报道。

text: Picture a huge public gathering, a sea of people like the Hajj to Mecca or an appearance by the Pope.
zh: 想象一下一个巨大的公众集会,人山人海,比如麦加朝觐或教皇露面这样的场合。

text: Think of the World Cup, the Olympics, a political event, or a rock concert?
zh: 想想世界杯、奥运会、政治活动,或者摇滚音乐会?

text: When thousands or even millions of people get together, what do you suppose is the biggest health concern?
zh: 当成千上万甚至数百万人聚集在一起时,你认为最大的健康隐患是什么?

text: Traditionally, doctors and public health officials were most concerned about the spread of infectious diseases, like influenza.
zh: 传统上,医生和公共卫生官员最担心的是传染病的传播,比如流感。

text: Robert Steffen, a researcher in Switzerland, says infectious diseases are still a concern.
zh: 瑞士研究员罗伯特·斯特芬表示,传染病仍然是一个隐患。

text: But he says injuries and hot weather are bigger threats at so -called mass gatherings.
zh: 但他说,在所谓的大型集会上,受伤和高温才是更大的威胁。

text: Mr. Steffen is a professor of travel medicine at the University of Zurich.
zh: 斯特芬先生是苏黎世大学的旅行医学教授。

text: He is the lead author of one of several new papers about health problems at mass gatherings in the journal Lancet, Infectious Diseases.
zh: 他是《柳叶刀·传染病》杂志上多篇关于大型集会健康问题新论文中一篇的第一作者。

text: Professor Stephan says children and older people have the highest risk of injury or other health problems at these events.
zh: 斯特芬教授说,在这类活动中,儿童和老年人受伤或出现其他健康问题的风险最高。

text: He says children are more at risk of getting crushed in stampedes, while older people are at higher risk from extreme heat.
zh: 他说,儿童更容易在踩踏事件中被挤压受伤,而老年人则更容易受到极端高温的伤害。

text: Stampedes and cases of crushing at mass gatherings have caused an estimated 7 ,000 deaths over the past 30 years.
zh: 在过去30年里,大型集会上的踩踏和挤压事件估计已造成7000人死亡。

text: The design of an area can play a part.
zh: 场地的设计也可能是一个因素。

text: There may be narrow passages or other choke points that too many people try to use at once.
zh: 可能存在狭窄的通道或其他瓶颈,导致太多人同时试图通过。

text: Professor Steffen says the mood of a crowd can also play a part.
zh: 斯特芬教授说,人群的情绪也可能起到作用。

text: For instance, if fireworks are suddenly launched within a football stadium, people can get scared and try to escape.
zh: 例如,如果在足球场内突然放起烟花,人们可能会受到惊吓,试图逃离。

text: He says organizers of large gatherings need to avoid creating conditions that might lead to panic, stampedes, and heat stroke.
zh: 他说,大型集会的组织者需要避免制造可能导致恐慌、踩踏和中暑的条件。

text: And he says they must be ready to give medical care.
zh: 他还说,他们必须做好提供医疗救助的准备。

text: So what advice does he have for people attending a large gathering?
zh: 那么他对参加大型集会的人有什么建议呢?

text: First, get any needed vaccinations before traveling.
zh: 首先,出行前要接种任何必要的疫苗。

text: Then, stay away from any large mass of people as much as possible.
zh: 其次,尽可能远离任何大规模人群。

text: Also, be careful with alcohol and drugs, which can increase the risk of injuries.
zh: 另外,要小心饮酒和用药,这些会增加受伤的风险。

text: And that's the VOA Special English Health Report,
zh: 以上就是美国之音特别英语健康报道。

text: available at voaspecialenglish .com, with MP3s, PDF files for e -readers, and podcasts.
zh: 你可以在voaspecialenglish.com上获取MP3、供电子阅读器使用的PDF文件以及播客。

text: You can also find our programs on iTunes.
zh: 你也可以在iTunes上找到我们的节目。

text: Have you ever been in a huge crowd and worried about your safety?
zh: 你是否曾身处巨大的人群中,担心过自己的安全?

text: Or did you feel fine?
zh: 还是感觉一切都好?

text: Post a comment on our website and tell us about your experience.
zh: 请在我们的网站上发表评论,和我们分享你的经历。

text: For VOA Special English, I'm Carolyn Presutti.
zh: 我是卡罗琳·普雷苏蒂,这里是美国之音特别英语。
